creator: ade@FreeBSD.org
text:
With autotools@ hat on:

Whilst an exp-run is obviously going to be useful, I'm more concerned 
about the run-time issues that this proposed change will bring to light.

Given that we have no easy way to test whether resultant binaries, 
intermediary libraries, and so forth will actually work correctly, I'm 
seriously hesitant to see this committed.

At least at the moment -- I can be persuaded otherwise of course, but 
sometimes these "small" changes have far-reaching consequences, 
particularly given that such an update is going to affect stable/7, 
stable/8, stable/9 and head across all supported architectures, which 
may well have subtle differences in how such linking occurs.

-aDe
